text: Herein we describe the synthesis of a new set of bis-Schiff base thiobarbituric acid (TBA) derivatives and their in vitro α-glucosidase inhibitory activities. The leads were identified by exploring the multi component synthesis of TBA derivatives by exploring the combinatory approach of reagents in aqueous medium. Structural verification of the compounds was done using various physicochemical methods of characterization. All the synthesized compounds showed considerable α-glucosidase inhibitory activities. Among these compounds, compound 8 (IC50 = 0.10 ± 0.05 µM) and compound 9 (IC50 = 0.13 ± 0.03 µM) showed significant inhibition than that of the standard acarbose (IC50 = 0.27 ± 0.04 µM) whereas compounds 5, 6, 7, 10 and 4 showed significant to good inhibition with IC50 values in the range of 0.32 ± 0.03-0.52 ± 0.02 µM. The bioactivity prediction of the synthesized compounds has been carried out by in silico docking method. The binding efficiency of the compounds to the target enzyme α-glucosidase and their active sites interactions were demonstrated by docking studies. The molecular orbitals of the synthesized compounds were explained by density functional theory (DFT) calculation using hybrid B3LYP functional with 6-311++G(d,p) basis set. Intramolecular H-bonding in the compounds were elucidated by DFT-d3 and reduced density gradient (RGD) analysis. Various reactivity parameters like HOMO-E, ΔN, ΔEi and the energy values of (2)3σg, E(T1u), E(T2u) were elucidated by performing TD-DFT at CAM-B3LYP/6-311++G(d,p) method.
explanation: The passage reads like a technical abstract with plausible data, but contains odd phrasing and unusual notation (e.g., 'multi component', 'HOMO-E', unusual TD-DFT terms) that often appear in AI-generated text. This yields moderate-to-high suspicion of AI authorship.
